items.description: All new endpoints require authentication (JWT RS256/ES256 validated, not HS256)
Authorization checked server-side for every resource operation — IDOR prevention confirmed
Server-side schema validation on all new inputs (Zod/Valibot/Yup/Joi)
Rate limiting configured on all new endpoints — per-user and per-IP
CORS origin allowlist reviewed — no wildcard on authenticated endpoints
Request size limits enforced — no unbounded body parsing
SSRF protection on any server-side HTTP client — block private IPs and metadata endpoints
Webhook signatures verified with HMAC-SHA256 and replay protection
OpenAPI spec updated for all new endpoints
CSRF protections present on all state-mutating browser-accessible endpoints
Error responses reviewed — no stack traces, internal paths, or schema details
Security events logged for all auth decisions — no PII or secrets in logs
JWT expiry enforced — access tokens max 15 minutes, refresh tokens rotated
No raw SQL string concatenation — parameterized queries or ORM used throughout
Mass assignment prevention — explicit field allowlists, not object spread from request body
Sensitive data (PII, credentials) not included in API responses unless required
API versioning strategy in place — old versions have defined deprecation timeline
Backend dependencies scanned — no CRITICAL CVEs unresolved
Secrets scan clean — no hardcoded credentials or API keys
SAST scan passed with no CRITICAL findings
Threat model completed and reviewed for this API surface change
Health/readiness endpoints do not expose sensitive version or config info
GraphQL introspection disabled in production; query depth limit ≤ 10 and complexity budget enforced to prevent resource exhaustion
Financial and state-changing endpoints protected against replay via idempotency keys or HMAC-timestamped signatures with a time-bound window ≤ 5 minutes
No user-controlled input passed to child_process.exec, execSync, spawn with shell:true, or any shell interpreter — allowlisted command templates only
File upload endpoints enforce MIME type server-side (not by extension), max file size, extension allowlist, and malware scanning; files stored outside web root
All sensitive equality checks (tokens, reset codes, HMAC digests, API keys) use crypto.timingSafeEqual — no string ===, indexOf, or includes comparisons

items.title: Second-order injection prevention reviewed — data retrieved from DB never passed unsanitized to SQL/template/shell sinks
SpEL/OGNL/MVEL expression parsers do not evaluate user-controlled input
Python pickle.loads/Marshal.load/PHP unserialize never called on user-supplied data
Java ObjectInputStream.readObject/XStream/Kryo deserialization never used with untrusted data
Elasticsearch query_string and script.source do not include user input
WebSocket message handlers validate all incoming data before passing to sinks
gRPC/Protobuf endpoints validate all message fields after deserialization
MongoDB query field values validated as scalar types — $gt/$regex stripped from user input
HTTP response splitting prevented — writeHead/headers.set never include user CRLF sequences
PDF/Office document generation libraries sanitize user input — no formula injection (=CMD, =DDE)
Dynamic property assignment using user-controlled keys forbidden — bracket notation obj[userKey] validated
JWT kid header sanitised before DB lookup or filesystem access
JWKS URI pinned to static allowlist — token-supplied jwks_uri not followed
OAuth client_secret not hardcoded in client-side code — public clients use PKCE only
Session identifiers never transmitted in URL query parameters
Session and reset tokens generated with ≥ 16 bytes (128 bits) of cryptographic randomness
Persistent remember-me tokens rotated on each use, stored hashed server-side, hard-expired ≤ 30 days
Password reset tokens deleted or nullified immediately after first use
Login and password-reset endpoints return identical responses for invalid user vs. invalid credential
bcrypt cost factor ≥ 12 for all password hashing operations
No all-zero or zero-filled IVs used in cipher operations — IVs generated with crypto.randomBytes
RSA key generation uses ≥ 2048 bits — no sub-2048 key sizes
Diffie-Hellman parameters ≥ 2048 bits — no weak DH groups (modp1/2/5)
TLS cipher suites require ECDHE or DHE — forward secrecy enforced
GraphQL resolver arguments never concatenated into raw SQL/NoSQL queries
GraphQL alias count limited per request (≤ 15) — batching-based enumeration and DoS prevented
Server-Sent Events streams strip \r\n from user-controlled data before writing
Referrer-Policy: no-referrer set on pages that receive OAuth tokens; tokens not in URL query params
Pagination parameters (limit, offset, page) validated with minimum 1, maximum cap, non-negative enforcement
